channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Did you show ‘negative sentiment’ for insurance companies after the UHC CEO shooting? Police were watching
item.description: When news broke that the United Healthcare CEO was shot in broad daylight early last month, outrage erupted online. But it wasn’t aimed at the assassin. Instead, it was directed at the broken U.S. healthcare system he represented.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How an AI-generated ‘expert’ sank into media deadlines
item.description: Ashley Abramson first came across Sophie Cress in a cold pitch to her work email. Cress was asking to be an expert source for any stories Abramson was working on as a freelance reporter.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: How the broligarchy is imitating Trump in more ways than one
item.description: Sooner or later, the politicians who most admire Donald Trump begin to emulate him. They adopt his populist rhetoric, specific diction, or aggressive tone. If they happen to be men, they might stuff their closets with navy suits and red ties. Some of them even start to stand like him.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FortiGate admins advised to run a compromise assessment
item.description: Network administrators with Fortinet’s FortiGate next generation firewall in their IT environments are being warned to thoroughly scrutinize systems for possible compromise, following last week’s dump of stolen configuration and VPN credentials by a threat actor.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Google Cracks Down on Fake Online Reviews
item.description: British regulators say Google has committed to taking tougher measures against fake online reviews, including stricter punishments for rogue reviewers and businesses that try to profit from them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sony's next flagship headphones leak in official docs, and now I'm getting excited
item.description: An FCC filing for the Sony WH-1000XM6 shows better Bluetooth and what looks like different construction too
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: South of Midnight looks absolutely stunning, and it's now got an official release date
item.description: South of Midnight was my personal highlight of the Xbox Developer Direct, and it's coming sooner than you might think.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: EUSPA adjudica a GMV la nueva versión del generador de datos del servicio HAS de Galileo
item.description: La Agencia de la Unión Europea para el Programa Espacial (EUSPA) ha adjudicado a la multinacional tecnológica GMV el desarrollo de una nueva versión del generador de datos de alta precisión (HADG, por sus siglas en inglés) del servicio HAS de Galileo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Corporate One enables immediate payments with data orchestration hub
item.description: Corporate One Federal Credit Union, headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, is a non-profit that’s provided investment, funding, and payment solutions to their members for nearly 75 years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI debuts AI agent Operator to transform web task automation
item.description: OpenAI has unveiled “Operator,” a new AI agent designed to perform web-based tasks, offering potential productivity enhancements for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Massachusetts Democrats ask regulators to open Trump meme coins probe
item.description: Two Massachusetts Democrats sent a letter to various regulators asking them to open an investigation into President Trump's and first lady Melania Trump's recently launched meme coins. The letter, signed by Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) and Rep. Jake Auchincloss (D-Mass.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rescinds Biden’s executive order on AI safety, as promised
item.description: Hours after returning to the White House, President Donald Trump made a symbolic mark on the future of artificial intelligence by repealing former President Joe Biden’s guardrails for the fast-developing technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech titans sound off on Trump's AI project
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok goes dark as US ban takes effect
item.description: Millions of TikTok users in the United States are no longer able to watch videos on the social media platform as a federal ban on the immensely popular app takes effect.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fake job interview emails installing hidden cryptocurrency mining malware
item.description: Scammers have a new trick where they pretend to be job recruiters to plant malicious apps on your devices. It all starts with a deceptive email.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: China gains ground
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5
